mnemonics.title: Four Pillars of OOP
mnemonics.content: E-A-I-P: Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism - Think 'Everyone Abhors Inefficient Programming' or 'Elephants Are In Pretty Small enclosures'

mnemonics.title: Class vs Object
mnemonics.content: CLASS = BLUEPRINT (plan), OBJECT = HOUSE (actual). Blueprint doesn't live in, house does. Class defines, Object occupies memory.

acronyms.usage: A paradigm organizing code around objects containing data and behavior
The four fundamental concepts/pillars of OOP

visualTips.visualization: Think of a cookie cutter (class) and cookies (objects). One cutter makes many cookies. Class is the template, objects are actual instances.
Think of a capsule (pill) containing medicine inside. Data and methods are bundled together like medicine in a capsule.
Think of a car dashboard - you see speedometer, fuel gauge but don't see complex engine mechanics. Only essential info is shown.
Think of family inheritance - children inherit properties (genes, house) from parents. Child class inherits from parent class.
Think of water - can be ice, liquid, or steam (many forms). Same substance (message/method) behaves differently in different states.

analogies.analogy: Class is like a recipe card, Object is like the actual cake made using that recipe. Recipe defines ingredients and steps; cake is the tangible product.
Like a bank vault - money (data) is protected inside, only authorized methods (bank staff procedures) can access it.
Like using a TV remote - you press buttons (interface) without knowing the internal circuitry (implementation).
Like a fruit tree - apple tree (derived class) inherits trunk and roots (base class) but adds apple-specific properties.
Like a person who is simultaneously a teacher, father, and son - same person different roles/behaviors in different contexts.
